target_area.description: Results Section 4.4 embodied-data sanity and official-scene stability handoff

change_summary: Reframed the final embodied-data gate as a sanity gate rather than a benchmark result.
Made the supported conclusion more explicit: the selected official stack can load, render, and evaluate converted scenes end-to-end, while broad embodied-navigation robustness remains unsupported.
Kept the official-scene infrastructure paragraph claim-bounded with the required '18 required paired runs' phrase and the guardrailed 'do not report an NVIDIA official-scene performance row' wording.

observations: Page 6 keeps the Section 4.4 text, Table 6 discussion, Figure 3 panel, and Figure 3 caption in the same broad vertical structure.
The earlier 'benchmark up-grade' visual break was removed by changing the wording to 'benchmark result'.
Page 7 table and companion figure placement remained stable after the page 6 prose change.

verification.command: rg -n "Overfull|Float too large|too large|LaTeX Warning: Float|Rerun to get|Warning:.*Label|Undefined references|multiply defined|Package lineno Warning" paper/venues/acl27/build/main.log || true

verification.evidence: ok=true; title and abstract match metadata; abstract_word_count=169
PDF text contains the new sanity-gate wording, selected-official-stack conclusion, and the guardrailed NVIDIA official-scene performance omission.

debugging_note: The first verification pass caught two wording regressions: replacing '18 required paired runs' broke a layout guardrail test, and changing 'do not report' to 'omit' broke the claim-boundary guardrail. Both were traced to explicit tests/checker rules and fixed before the final verification pass.
workspace_note: The repository had unrelated pre-existing modified paper and table files before this round. This evidence record only claims the round27 Section 4.4 polish in results.tex plus the new record/evidence files.
